2604.07054.title: Sell More, Play Less: Benchmarking LLM Realistic Selling Skill
2604.07054.paper_summary: Sales dialogues require multi-turn, goal-directed persuasion under asymmetric incentives, which makes them a challenging setting for large language models (LLMs). Yet existing dialogue benchmarks rarely measure deal progression and outcomes. We introduce SalesLLM, a bilingual (ZH/EN) benchmark derived from realistic applications covering Financial Services and Consumer Goods, built from 30,074 scripted configurations and 1,805 curated multi-turn scenarios with controllable difficulty and personas. We propose a fully automatic evaluation pipeline that combines (i) an LLM-based rater for sales-process progress, and (ii) fine-tuned BERT classifiers for end-of-dialogue buying intent. To improve simulation fidelity, we train a user model, CustomerLM, with SFT and DPO on 8,000 crowdworker-involved sales conversations, reducing role inversion from 17.44% (GPT-4o) to 8.8%. SalesLLM scores correlate strongly with expert human ratings (Pearson r=0.98). Experiments across 15 mainstream LLMs reveal substantial variability: top-performance LLMs are competitive with human-level performance while the less capable ones are worse than human. SalesLLM serves as a scalable benchmark for developing and evaluating outcome-oriented sales agents.
2604.07054.paper_summary_zh: 銷售對話需要在不對稱激勵下進行多輪、目標導向的說服，這使得它們成為大型語言模型（LLMs）的一個挑戰性環境。<lb/>然而，現有的對話基準很少衡量交易進展和結果。<lb/>我們介紹了SalesLLM，一個雙語（ZH/EN）基準，源自涵蓋金融服務和消費品的現實應用，基於30,074個腳本配置和1,805個經過策劃的多輪場景，具有可控的難度和角色設定。<lb/>我們提出了一個完全自動的評估管道，結合了（i）基於LLM的評分者來評估銷售過程進展，以及（ii）經過微調的BERT分類器來評估對話結束時的購買意圖。<lb/>為了提高模擬的真實性，我們訓練了一個用戶模型CustomerLM，使用SFT和DPO在8,000個涉及人群工作者的銷售對話中，將角色反轉率從17.44%（GPT-4o）降低到8.8%。<lb/>SalesLLM的分數與專家人類評分強烈相關（Pearson r=0.98）。<lb/>在15個主流LLM上的實驗顯示出顯著的變異性：表現最佳的LLM與人類水平的表現具有競爭力，而能力較弱的LLM則不如人類。<lb/>SalesLLM作為一個可擴展的基準，用於開發和評估以結果為導向的銷售代理。
2604.07054.author: Xuanbo Su et.al.
2604.07054.authors: Xuanbo Su, Wenhao Hu, Le Zhan, Yanqi Yang, Leo Huang
